steps.title: Determine the direction of the trend
steps.content: Graph all four candidate lines in Desmos and compare them to the scatterplot. The data trend upward steeply, so the correct line should have positive slope and should not be nearly flat.

steps.title: Distinguish C from D using the slope magnitude
steps.content: The scatter plot shows a steep upward trend, not a nearly horizontal one. A slope of 0.2 would produce a nearly flat line across the full range of x; a slope of 1.80 produces a line that rises roughly 9 units over 5 units of x. The data clearly follows the steeper pattern, confirming D over C.

steps.title: Spot-check with a representative point
steps.content:
Using the point (5, 9) visible near the center of the plot:
Choice D: y = 1.80(5) + 0.2 = 9.0 + 0.2 = 9.2 — within 0.2 of 9. ✓
Choice C: y = 0.2(5) + 1.8 = 1.0 + 1.8 = 2.8 — far below the actual value of 9. ✗
That matches choice D — D is correct.
